|timeline=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref>When analysing ''The Duty'' against the ''[[Purge (comic book)|Purge]]'', ''[[Purge – Seconds to Die]]'', ''[[Purge – The Hidden Blade]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Lost Command|Darth Vader and the Lost Command]]'' story arcs in relation to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] obsession with hunting down all living [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], ''The Duty'''s timeline is somewhat ambiguous. The [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor's]] increasing rebukes of Vader's obsession, as a background narrative, that further evolves throughout the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times|Dark Times]]'' series provides contextualisation. Hence, ''The Duty'' must occur earlier rather than later in Vader and Darth Sidious' story arc.</ref>

"'''The Duty'''" is a short comic featured in ''[[Star Wars Tales 12]]''.

==Plot summary==
It is the time of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. One [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] by the name of [[Gira]] is the last man standing in defending a group of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] attack. As the Padawans make their way safely to an escape ship, Gira dispatches the last of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. His success is cut short when he encounters [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] who forces him towards the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Gira defiantly refuses to give in until the safety of the padawans is jejeopardisedHowever, Vader kills the Padawans anyway, and Gira commits suicide.
{{Plot|comic}}
